title: eremophila accrescens
intro: Eremophila accrescens, also known as the large leaved emu bush, is a flowering shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and large, elliptical leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. The health benefits associated with Eremophila accrescens include wound healing, anti-inflammatory effects, and antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of Eremophila accrescens are typically made from the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as a tea, infused into a topical ointment, or used as a tincture.
